ground_truth.title: What is a smartphone? | Free Online Course | Be Connected
ground_truth.main_content: This course will teach you what a smartphone is and how to use one. It is designed for those who are new to using a smartphone. A smartphone is a lot like a tablet computer, but small enough to fit in your pocket and able to make or receive calls. There are two main types of smartphone: Android and Apple iPhone. They look similar and do the same things, just a bit differently. Like a tablet, a smartphone has a battery inside that can last several hours before running out of power. Connecting the smartphone to mains power lets you recharge the battery. Smartphones often have a large screen that is a touchscreen, and you do most things, including making phone calls, by tapping it with your finger. The main screen of a smartphone is called the home screen, just like on a tablet. It has a lot of small symbols called applications, or apps for short. Apps are small computer programs that let you do a range of things on your smartphone. There are apps for games, the weather or listening to the radio. To make a call, tap the phone button, a number pad will appear, tap in the number with your finger, then press the call button. This course includes three activities: 'Get to know your smartphone' (6 min), 'Using your smartphone' (6 min), and 'Selecting a mobile plan that's right for you' (5 min).

There are two main types of smartphone: Android and Apple iPhone. They look similar and do the same things, just a bit differently.
Smartphones often have a large screen. Like a tablet, this is a touchscreen, and you do most things, including making phone calls, by tapping it with your finger.
The main screen of a smartphone is called the home screen, just like on a tablet. It has a lot of small symbols called applications, or apps for short.
To make a call, tap the phone button. A number pad will appear. Tap in the number with your finger, then press the call button.
